steps.title: Step 1
steps.instruction: Gather two Fabric A rectangles two Fabric A squares two Fabric B rectangles two Fabric B squares Note: Fabric A will now be referred to as the Background Fabric, and Fabrics B-O will be referred to as the Print Fabrics. From Fabric A, cut: twenty-three 3-1/2” x WOF strips. Subcut: ninety 3-1/2” x 7” rectangles ninety 3-1/2” squares. Mark a diagonal on the back side of each square. From each of Fabrics B-L, cut: two 3-1/2” x WOF strips. Subcut: six 3-1/2” x 7” rectangles six 3-1/2” squares. Mark a diagonal on the back side of each square. From each of Fabrics M-O, cut: three 3-1/2” x WOF strips. Subcut: eight 3-1/2” x 7” rectangles eight 3-1/2” squares. Mark a diagonal on the back side of each square. From the Binding Fabric, cut: seven 2-1/2” x WOF strips Notes Before You Begin Assemble the Blocks Cutting Instructions
steps.clarifiedInstruction: Gather two Fabric A rectangles two Fabric A squares two Fabric B rectangles two Fabric B squares Note: Fabric A will now be referred to as the Background Fabric, and Fabrics B-O will be referred to as the Print Fabrics. From Fabric A, cut: twenty-three 3-1/2” x Width of Fabric (WOF) strips. Subcut: ninety 3-1/2” x 7” rectangles ninety 3-1/2” squares. Mark a diagonal on the back side of each square. From each of Fabrics B-L, cut: two 3-1/2” x Width of Fabric (WOF) strips. Subcut: six 3-1/2” x 7” rectangles six 3-1/2” squares. Mark a diagonal on the back side of each square. From each of Fabrics M-O, cut: three 3-1/2” x Width of Fabric (WOF) strips. Subcut: eight 3-1/2” x 7” rectangles eight 3-1/2” squares. Mark a diagonal on the back side of each square. From the Binding Fabric, cut: seven 2-1/2” x Width of Fabric (WOF) strips Notes Before You Begin Assemble the Blocks Cutting Instructions

steps.title: Step 2
steps.instruction: Place a Background square at the top edge of a Print rectangle, right sides together. Note the orientation of the marked diagonal line. Sew on the marked line, then trim away the excess fabric, leaving a 1/4” seam allowance. Press. Repeat to make a second unit like this.  -Read through all of the instructions before beginning. -All of the seam allowances are 1/4” unless otherwise noted. -Press seam allowances open unless otherwise noted. -Width of fabric (WOF) is equal to at least 42” wide. -Length of fabric has been abbreviated to LOF. -Right sides together has been abbreviated to RST. -Remember to measure twice and cut once!
steps.clarifiedInstruction: Place a Background square at the top edge of a Print rectangle, right sides together. Note the orientation of the marked diagonal line. Sew on the marked line, then trim away the excess fabric, leaving a 1/4” seam allowance. Press. Repeat to make a second unit like this.  -Read through all of the instructions before beginning. -All of the seam allowances are 1/4” unless otherwise noted. -Press seam allowances open unless otherwise noted. -Width of fabric (WOF) is equal to at least 42” wide. -Length of fabric has been abbreviated to Length of Fabric (LOF). -Right sides together has been abbreviated to Right Sides Together (RST). -Remember to measure twice and cut once!

steps.title: Step 3
steps.instruction: Place a Print square at the top edge of a Background rectangle, right sides together. Note the orientation of the marked diagonal line and how it is the mirror image of the marked line in
steps.clarifiedInstruction: Place a Print square at the top edge of a Background rectangle, right sides together. Note the orientation of the marked diagonal line and how it is the mirror image of the marked line in

steps.title: Step 2
steps.instruction: Sew on the marked line, then trim away the excess fabric, leaving a 1/4” seam allowance. Press. Repeat to make a second unit like this.
steps.clarifiedInstruction: Sew on the marked line, then trim away the excess fabric, leaving a 1/4” seam allowance. Press. Repeat to make a second unit like this.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.instruction: Arrange the units into two rows of two. Note the fabric placement and orientation in the Block Assembly Diagram.
steps.clarifiedInstruction: Arrange the units into two rows of two. Note the fabric placement and orientation in the Block Assembly Diagram.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.instruction: Sew the units together to form the rows. Press the seams to the left in the top row and to the right in the bottom row.
steps.clarifiedInstruction: Sew the units together to form the rows. Press the seams to the left in the top row and to the right in the bottom row.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.instruction: Sew the rows together, nesting the seams and pressing the seams to one side to form the block. The Block should measure 6-1/2” x 13-1/2”. Repeat to make: three Blocks from each of Fabrics B-L four Blocks from each of Fabrics M-O.
steps.clarifiedInstruction: Sew the rows together, nesting the seams and pressing the seams to one side to form the block. The Block should measure 6-1/2” x 13-1/2”. Repeat to make: three Blocks from each of Fabrics B-L four Blocks from each of Fabrics M-O.

steps.title: Step 7
steps.instruction: Arrange the blocks into five rows of nine Blocks. Note the fabric placement and block orientation in the Quilt Assembly Diagram.
steps.clarifiedInstruction: Arrange the blocks into five rows of nine Blocks. Note the fabric placement and block orientation in the Quilt Assembly Diagram.

steps.title: Step 8
steps.instruction: Sew the blocks together to form rows. Press the rows to the left in the odd numbered rows and to the right in the even numbered rows.
steps.clarifiedInstruction: Sew the blocks together to form rows. Press the rows to the left in the odd numbered rows and to the right in the even numbered rows.

steps.title: Step 9
steps.instruction: Sew the rows together to form the quilt center. Press the row seams Your quilt top is complete! Baste, quilt, bind and enjoy!
